seoTitle: Lower Longley vs Lower Marshes — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lower Longley and Lower Marshes. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lower Longley vs Lower Marshes: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Longley and Lower Marshes.
heading: Lower Longley vs Lower Marshes — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Longley, Tasmania and Lower Marshes, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Marshes has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Lower Longley (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Longley or Lower Marshes safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Longley has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Lower Marshes scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Longley scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Marshes scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Longley or Lower Marshes?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Marshes has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
